title: chamaemelum × valentinum
intro: Chamaemelum × valentinum, also known as Corn Marigold or Valerian Chamomile, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the daisy family and is a cross between Chamaemelum nobile and Anthemis nobilis. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and act as an antispasmodic. The health benefits of Chamaemelum × valentinum include its potential to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. Chamaemelum × valentinum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
